comments.post_body:
5 dokaza da je Rajder iz Patrolnih šapa toksični mikromenadžer:

1. Ne vrednuje tuđe vreme. Na sastanke saziva sve članove tima, umesto samo one koji će učestvovati u akciji.

2. Govori drugima šta da rade, umesto da njima to prepusti. Kao da Rabl ne zna šta treba da radi kašikom bagera ili Skaj kukom helikoptera.

3. Favorizuje određene članove tima. U ogromnom broju akcija učestvuju Čejs i Skaj, dok Maršal dođe na red u svakoj 10. epizodi (čekali smo pet dana da čujemo koja je Maršalova rečenica kad ga Rajder prozove)

4. Ne poštuje slobodno vreme članova svog tima. Često ih zove čak i po mraku, očekujući da se oni momentalno odazovu. To stvara ogroman stres kod Šapa.

5. Daje klijentima obećanja bez prethodne saglasnosti članova svog tima. Svakom ko ga nazove kaže da odmah dolaze i da će biti rešeno, čime Šapama ne ostavlja nimalo prostora za pregovore i stalno ih baca u vatru.

(Do temperature je, nije do mene)
comments.context_summary: The post discusses 5 reasons why Ryder from Paw Patrol is a toxic micromanager, including not respecting team members' time, favoring certain members, and making promises to clients without consulting the team. The commenter is responding directly to the post, likely agreeing with or adding to the points made about Ryder's management style.

comments.comment_body: The output I pasted is actually based when max_length=15, but even with smaller numbers it can look pretty good.
comments.post_body:
I would have never thought that creating a simple text prediction software is that easy. After watching one of the Veritasium videos on Markov chains I got inspired by the story how Markov himself created manual and tedios "network" based on the text of Eugene Onegin by Pushkin. I was suspicious that such a simple model with just checking last few charachers can produce text that, of course being non-sensical, seems very legit on the first glance.

So, I've spent an hour of so implementing this in C++, fed it with some English texts and I've got some really decent results, for example, looking like this:

"That wild yell seemed to come a violent puff of wind, and the door to the winding stair blew to with a shock that set the dust from the lintels flying. When I ran to push it open, I found that it was hopelessly fast. I was again a prisoner, and that if I wished it I could have no choice. The Count saw his victory in my bow, and his mastery in the trouble of my face, for he said almost joyously:, you don't count now; the Master is at hand. The attendant thinks it is some sudden form of religious mania at once might be angerous. I got a ladder myself, and crossing the wall, dropped down on the other side of a true love episode of which I know one side already...."

The C++ code is as simple as this https://lnkd.in/gxEWHAYJ

Anyways, real LLMs are way more complex and they base their predicitons on words, or more preciely tokens and have multiple layers of decision makings. However, a simple code like this can still produce interesting results.
comments.context_summary: The commenter was inspired by a Veritasium video on Markov chains and created a simple text prediction software in C++, which produced surprisingly decent results, and is now sharing their experience and code, highlighting the difference between their basic model and more complex large language models. They demonstrate the potential of simple models to generate interesting text, despite their limitations.

- AI je ipak značajna pomoć, kao da imam još jednog programera u timu.

They find AI to be a significant help, likening it to having an additional team member.

comments.comment_body: U principu tačno. Teško je ukomponovati da ljudi koji rade zajedno baš sede jedni pored drugih, možda i nemoguče. Danas radim sa jednim kolegom i saradjujem dosta, a za dve nedelje će mi glavni saradnik biti neko potuno drugi.
comments.post_body:
Zašto je open space sr…nje? Ili u prevodu, kada mogu da dobijem bolje slušalice?

Petnaest godina sam vodio IT kompanije.
Deset godina pre toga radio sam u njima, kao inženjer.
Za sve to vreme nagledao sam se različitih kancelarijskih formata, ali jedna tema se nike menjala, open space. Uvek je  imao istu priču: povećaće kolaboraciju, ljudi će više razgovarati, biće otvoreniji jedni prema drugima i efikasnost rada na projektima će porasti.

Ali stvarnost je potpuno drugačija. Da li mogu da dobijem bolje slušalice i mikrofon koji izoluje buku, da li mogu da se premestim u drugu kancelariju, da li mogu intervju sa klijentom da odradim od kuće, potreban mi je fokus. 

U tim prostorima gde bi trebalo da se „svi čuju“, ljudi se sve manje slušaju.
Sede jedni pored drugih, a ne kažu ni „dobro jutro“. Kada sam prvi put to video davne 2008.godine, nisam mogao da verujem. Sedim u open space kancelariji za pedesetak ljudi, u drugom gradu, i gledam, ulaze jedan po jedan, i samo re retki javljaju jedni drugima. Oni koji sede jedni do drugih, naravno, najšešće.
Neki ne znaju imena svojih kolega, niti imaju predstavu ko uopšte radi sa kojim tehnologijama i na kojim projektima.
Zamišljen kao otvoren, zajednički prostor, open space se pretvorio u zatvorenost, u prostor gde se svako brani od drugih svojim slušalicama, ekranima i pogledom u pod.

Menadžment voli da kaže da zidovi prave barijere.
Istina je da barijere prave ljudi.
Ako ti je organizaciona kultura zatvorena, ako nema poverenja, ako ljudi ne osećaju potrebu da se diskutuju jedni sa drugima, skidanje zidova neće promeniti ništa osim troškova za molera.
I tu leži ironija: kada saradnja ne postoji, nije problem u rasporedu stolova, nego u onome kako vodiš firmu, i kakvu organizacionu kulturu živi ta kompanija. Ono što sam naučio kroz karijeru i kroz istraživanje u pripremi disertacije je da je organizaciona kultura kao voda. Ukoliko je ti ne oblikuješ, ne menadžuješ, sa stavom, “ma sve će to da se desi samo od sebe”, organizaciona kultura nadje svoj put, i svoj način da se uoblikuje. Često, ne u ono što menadžment kompanije želi.

Zašto se i dalje guramo u open space, zašto se to prodaje kao „rešenje za budućnost rada“, i zašto menadžment uporno brani koncept koji očigledno ne funkcioniše?
Možda zato što je lakše meriti kvadrate nego poverenje.
Ali pravo pitanje je: da li menadžment zaista veruje da ljudi rade bolje kada sede zajedno, ili je to samo mit koji niko ne sme da prizna da ne funkcioniše? Ja nisam smeo, priznajem.
comments.context_summary: The author, with 25 years of experience in IT companies, discusses the ineffectiveness of open space offices in promoting collaboration and communication among employees, and argues that the problem lies in the organizational culture, not the physical layout. The author believes that a closed organizational culture, lack of trust, and poor management are the main barriers to collaboration, and that removing walls will not solve these issues.

comments.comment_body: Preteruju firme, ali preteruju i kandidati. Bilo i bice.
comments.post_body:
Nisam nešto posebno vrijedna sa statusima, ali sjetih se doživljaja koji ne mogu zadržati za sebe.

Razgovor za posao. 
Firma je, citiram: 
- respektabilna, 
- renomirana, 
- zauzima vodeće mjesto na prostoru na kojem posluje….

Hvale se oni. Hvalim se i ja. Red je.

I onda trenutak istine.

- Imate li pitanja?
- Imam. Za koju platu se radi?

- To vam sad ne možemo reći. To je za drugi krug.

Naravno da jeste. Prvi krug je da vidiš jesam li očajna.

Zoveš me usred radnog vremena, natjeraš me da lažem šefu, čačkaš mi biografiju kao arheološko nalazište i onda mistika oko plate.

- Ali možda vi imate neku predstavu i šta mislite o minimalnoj plati?

- Imam predstavu, naravno. Ako ste, kao što ste naveli, renomirani, respektabilni i društveno odgovorni, sigurno ne biste dozvolili da ljudi rade za crkavicu. Sve ispod (kažem neku normalnu cifru) je crkavica. Ja imam 19 godina škole, 25 godina staža i ne radim za crkavicu. A to minimalno što zovete plata, to nije plata vec sramota. Ugodan dan.

I dok izlazim, mislim samo jedno:
Nije problem što nemate para.
Problem je što nemate stida.

✅ Sve je više oglasa za posao u kojima se poslodavci opisuju kao savršeno radno okruženje, dok realni uslovi ostaju nejasni ili se kasnije pokažu drugačijima. Takva praksa dovodi kandidate u zabludu i narušava povjerenje na tržištu rada.
Društvena odgovornost počinje iskrenošću: jasno navedite platu, radno vrijeme, očekivanja i izazove posla. To je pošten odnos prema ljudima koji ulažu svoje vrijeme i karijeru. I ne očekujte da ljudi rade za kikiriki!!
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about a job interview where the company, despite claiming to be reputable and socially responsible, refused to disclose the salary, leading to a discussion on the importance of transparency and honesty in job postings. The commenter emphasizes that withholding such information is misleading and undermines trust in the job market.

comments.comment_body: Za kratkorocno parkiranje ovo je mozda ok, ali ne znam ko bi hteo da parkira svoja kola ispod biljke koja ispusta najlepljivije materije, osim ako nisu vestacke biljke. Pritom mozda je i AI kao sto neko rece u drugom komentaru.
comments.post_body:
Japan je na drugoj planeti; mi čak ne možemo ni da kopiramo jer sebe smatramo previše pametnim. Cementiramo i uništavamo zelenilo😇😪

🌿🚗 JAPAN: PARKINGI SE TRANSFORMIŠU U VISEĆE BAŠTE 🌸🍃
Nije dovoljno biti efikasan. Morate biti zeleni, prozračni i poštovati životnu sredinu.

U Japanu, modernost se susreće sa prirodom na iznenađujući način: javni parkinzi su prekriveni cvetnim zelenim krovovima, stvarajući bujne prostore koji kombinuju funkcionalnost, estetiku i poštovanje životne sredine.

📍 Ove „viseće bašte“ nisu jednostavne dekoracije:
one su konkretan model urbane održivosti, koji se može ponoviti u našim gradovima!

🌺 PREDNOSTI OVIH ZELENIH PARKING MESTA:

✅ Smanjenje gradske toplote – Biljke snižavaju temperature i suprotstavljaju se efektu toplotnog ostrva.

✅ Prirodna hladovina za automobile – Manje korišćenje klima uređaja i veća udobnost.

✅ Urbani biodiverzitet – Bezbedna utočišta za pčele, leptire i oprašivače, sve više ugrožene urbanizacijom.
✅ Bolji kvalitet vazduha – Biljke apsorbuju ugljen-dioksid, filtriraju prašinu i toksine.
✅ Estetska vrednost – Boje i mirisi koji pretvaraju parking u urbanu oazu.
✅ Psihološko blagostanje – Vizuelni kontakt sa prirodom, čak i na najneočekivanijim mestima, smanjuje stres i anksioznost.

🧠 Inicijativa pod nazivom (plutajuće bašte) koja pokazuje kako inovacija može ići ruku pod ruku sa zaštitom životne sredine.
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to an article about Japan's innovative approach to urban sustainability, where public parking lots are being transformed into green spaces with flower-covered roofs, and is expressing frustration that their own country is not following suit. The commenter is highlighting the benefits of such green spaces, including reduced urban heat and improved air quality.

comments.comment_body: Sounds too optimistic.
comments.post_body:
AI is UNLIKELY to steal your job. 

Here’s why ⬇️

Technological innovation has historically transformed jobs rather than eliminated them.

In 1950, the U.S. Census listed 270 occupations. 

Since then, only one job has been completely eliminated due to automation: elevator operators.

Software engineering is another great example. 

Google has revolutionized how engineers work—so much so that some jokingly call themselves "professional Googlers." 

But instead of replacing engineers, Google has made them more productive.

So rather than fearing our new robot overlords, we should welcome them.

Do you think AI will eliminate your job? 

Let us know in the comments below.

—
📌 Free 7-day AI strategy crash course:
dub.sh/ai-strat-post-cta
comments.context_summary: The post discusses how AI is unlikely to steal jobs, citing historical examples of technological innovation transforming jobs rather than eliminating them, and the commenter is responding to this optimistic view of AI's impact on employment. The commenter's thoughts on whether AI will eliminate their job are being solicited in response to the post's argument.

comments.comment_body: It's Mileva, not Milena.
comments.post_body:
Milena Einstein with her first husband. She contributed significantly to special relativity. Today we might know only him.

Nobody made it clearer than Albert Einstein himself that [the couple] collaborated on special relativity when he wrote to [his first wife] Mileva on 27 March 1901: “How happy and proud I will be when the two of us together will have brought our work on relative motion to a victorious conclusion.”

Quoted from: https://lnkd.in/e7pqfny6

#womeninscience #forgottenlife
comments.context_summary: The post discusses the significant contribution of Milena Einstein, Albert Einstein's first wife, to his theory of special relativity, highlighting how her role has been overlooked. The commenter is responding to the post, which quotes Albert Einstein himself acknowledging their collaborative work on relative motion.

I am proud to be part of an effort to bring Microsoft Edge browser to Linux: https://lnkd.in/gYybdrN 

Not only Edge runs on Linux, you can sign in to Edge profile using your Microsoft Account, and have access to your history, favorites, and personalization.

If you'd like to come and join us in shaping the future of computing, apply here: https://lnkd.in/gnfUcKH

#linux #microsoft #edge #azure #identity
comments.context_summary: The commenter is responding to a post about Microsoft Edge browser being brought to Linux, highlighting its features such as signing in with a Microsoft Account and accessing personalized data. The post also invites others to join the effort in shaping the future of computing.

comments.comment_body: Koliko sam video zavod za udzbenike ima isto sav materijal za osnovnu skolu dostupan na svom sajtu.
comments.post_body: Ovo je zaista bitna stvar. Ne sumnjam da će nekako biti ugušena raznim interesima, ali i dalje je jako bitna, bitna kao ideja, kao iskorak u načinu razmišljanja. Ne sme baš svaki aspekt života da bude pokretan parama.
comments.context_summary: The commenter agrees that the post's topic is crucial and should not be suppressed by various interests, emphasizing its importance as an idea and a step forward in thinking. They believe that not every aspect of life should be driven by money.
